title: WordPress REST API — Posts & Content
description: External API consumer creates, reads, updates, and deletes posts and revisions via REST. Covers WP_REST_Posts_Controller, WP_REST_Revisions_Controller, and WP_REST_Blocks_Controller.

actors.name: API Consumer
actors.description: Any external client (editor, headless app, plugin) calling the WP REST API

actors.name: Authenticated Editor
actors.description: Logged-in user with edit_posts / create_posts / delete_post capabilities

actors.name: Administrator
actors.description: User with edit_others_posts and publish_posts caps; required for sticky/author assignment

actors.name: WordPress Core
actors.description: Internal subsystem: WP_Query, wp_insert_post, wp_update_post, wp_delete_post, revision engine

use_cases.name: List Posts
use_cases.permission_check: get_items_permissions_check — requires edit_posts cap only when context=edit; public otherwise
use_cases.notes: Supports full WP_Query params: filter, search, orderby, per_page, page, include/exclude, status, author, categories, tags

use_cases.name: Get Single Post
use_cases.permission_check: get_item_permissions_check → get_post() → check_read_permission(); password field validated via hash_equals
use_cases.notes: Password-protected posts: must supply ?password= or have edit_post cap. Returns Link: alternate header pointing to frontend permalink.

use_cases.name: Create Post
use_cases.permission_check: create_item_permissions_check — requires create_posts cap; edit_others_posts for author override; edit_others_posts/publish_posts for sticky
use_cases.handler: create_item() → prepare_item_for_database() → wp_insert_post() → handle_featured_media / handle_terms / meta.update_value → prepare_item_for_response()
use_cases.notes: Returns 201 Created + Location header. Supports batch (v1).

use_cases.name: Update Post
use_cases.permission_check: update_item_permissions_check → check_update_permission(post); blocks author/sticky changes without appropriate caps
use_cases.handler: update_item() → prepare_item_for_database() → wp_update_post() → handle_featured_media / handle_terms / meta.update_value → prepare_item_for_response()
use_cases.notes: Slug uniqueness enforced for draft/pending status. Supports batch (v1).

use_cases.name: Delete Post
use_cases.handler: delete_item() — trash if EMPTY_TRASH_DAYS>0 (wp_trash_post), or permanent delete if ?force=true (wp_delete_post)
use_cases.notes: Returns {deleted:true, previous:{...}} on permanent delete; trashed post data on soft delete.

use_cases.name: List Revisions
use_cases.notes: Requires parent post to exist and match parent_post_type. Returns X-WP-Total / X-WP-TotalPages headers.

use_cases.name: Get Single Revision
use_cases.notes: Schema inherits title/content/excerpt/guid properties from parent controller's schema.

use_cases.name: Delete Revision
use_cases.permission_check: delete_item_permissions_check → current_user_can('delete_post', parent_id) AND current_user_can('delete_post', revision_id)
use_cases.handler: delete_item() — requires ?force=true (no trash support for revisions) → wp_delete_post(id, true)
use_cases.notes: Revisions do NOT support trashing. force=true is mandatory.

use_cases.name: CRUD Reusable Block
use_cases.permission_check: check_read_permission() — requires read_post cap (mapped to edit_posts by default) + parent check_read_permission
use_cases.handler: Inherits all CRUD from WP_REST_Posts_Controller; overrides check_read_permission, filter_response_by_context, get_item_schema
use_cases.notes: Blocks stored as wp_block post type. title.rendered and content.rendered removed from response/schema — only raw markup exposed. raw context available in both view and edit.

key_relationships: WP_REST_Posts_Controller depends_on WP_REST_Post_Meta_Fields (this->meta for meta CRUD)
WP_REST_Revisions_Controller depends_on WP_REST_Posts_Controller (parent_controller, fallback if no custom controller)
WP_REST_Revisions_Controller calls parent_controller.get_item_schema() to inherit title/content/excerpt/guid schema fields
WP_REST_Blocks_Controller extends WP_REST_Posts_Controller (post_type=wp_block, overrides read permission and schema)
All controllers use WordPress capability system: current_user_can(cap, post_id)
All controllers call WP_Query (get_items), wp_insert_post / wp_update_post / wp_delete_post / wp_trash_post for DB ops

permission_capability_map.read_post: public for published; edit_post for password-protected/draft
permission_capability_map.delete_revision: delete_post on parent AND delete_post on revision

business_rules: Posts support soft-delete (trash) when EMPTY_TRASH_DAYS > 0; hard delete requires ?force=true
Revisions never support trash — ?force=true always required
Password-protected posts: consumer supplies ?password= or must have edit_post cap
Slug uniqueness for draft/pending is enforced by converting to 'publish' status for wp_unique_post_slug()
Blocks (wp_block) expose only raw title/content — rendered fields intentionally omitted from both schema and response
Revision schema inherits title/content/excerpt/guid from parent post type's schema (dynamic composition)
Batched operations supported on Posts (allow_batch v1)
Namespace defaults to wp/v2; rest_base and rest_namespace are configurable per post type object
hooks_fired: rest_insert_{post_type}(post, request, creating) — after wp_insert_post / wp_update_post
rest_after_insert_{post_type}(post, request, creating) — after all field/term/meta updates
rest_prepare_{post_type}(response, post, request) — filter on prepare_item_for_response
rest_prepare_revision(response, post, request) — filter on revision prepare
rest_revision_query(args, request) — filter WP_Query args for revisions
